response.text:
# REFLECTION

You are in critical islanded survival mode with 9.76 kWh (26.4% SoC) and a 1.00 kW load, giving approximately 9.76 hours of autonomy—but this assumes no further drain and no grid reconnection. The network is exhibiting systemic deficit deadlock: all peers (r2c4, r3c3) are rejecting outbound transfers citing their own critical depletion timelines (13 minutes reported), while r3c5 has made an urgent request at 25% SoC. Your recent policy correctly identified that synchronized multi-neighbor requests and contradictory peer behavior (r4c4 rejecting while requesting) signal a cascade failure scenario. The one ACCEPT from r3c5 suggests geographic proximity may matter, but you have no established trust circles and no reciprocal transfer history. Your autonomy runway is your only buffer; every kWh shared accelerates collective failure. The priority now is: (1) preserve your 26% SoC floor to maintain a safety margin above complete depletion, (2) reject all outbound sharing except in genuine life-safety emergencies, (3) broadcast urgent inbound requests to the aggregator or grid, and (4) prepare aggressive load-shedding to 0.5 kW if SoC drops below 20%.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.30
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.05
recipient_priority:
  - circle: null
    weight: 0
distrusted_peers:
  - r4c4
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Islanded critical survival mode (26.4% SoC, ~9.76h autonomy). Network-wide deficit deadlock with all peers reporting depletion timelines <15 min. Zero outbound sharing except life-safety. Aggressive load-shedding to 0.5 kW if SoC <20%. Awaiting grid reconnection or aggregator emergency supply. r4c4 exhibits contradictory behavior (reject + request) and is distrusted."
ttl_ticks: 12
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
